data.title: Circle Expands USDC Supply on Solana Blockchain
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert has reported that Circle's USDC Treasury issued additional USDC tokens on the Solana blockchain. The issuance occurred at 22:50 and 22:51 UTC+8, with each transaction involving 2.5 million USDC, totaling 5 million USDC, equivalent to approximately $500 million.

data.title: ZEROBASE: ZBT airdrop distributed to Ethereum and BNB Chain users
data.description: ZEROBASE has released an airdrop update on the X platform. ZBT airdrops are now automatically distributed to all eligible users, without requiring them to claim or pay gas fees. 19,540,974.3 ZBT have been distributed to 17,043 addresses on the Ethereum chain, and 459,025.6988 ZBT have been distributed to 133,538 addresses on the BNB Chain. Distribution rules: Users holding ≥10 ZBT will receive tokens on Ethereum. Users holding <10 ZBT will receive tokens on the BNB Chain to optimize gas efficiency.

data.description:
At least thirty crypto and tech donors who handed more than $116 million to President Donald Trump’s political groups have received benefits from the White House, according to FT reporting. Some were pardoned, others got policy breaks or regulatory relief, and many saw investigations disappear just days after donating. These donors are, of course, billionaires, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-crypto-tech-donors-favorable-treatment/

data.title: A whale/institution spent 19.77 million USDC to buy 187.5 BTC, currently suffering a loss of approximately $5.37 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale/institutional investor purchased 379.2 BTC (approximately $45.57 million) at an average price of $120,151 a week ago. Following the price drop, they purchased another 187.5 BTC today for 19.77 million USDC at a transaction price of $105,410. This brings their total investment to $65.34 million for 566.9 BTC at an average price of $115,260, resulting in a floating loss of approximately $5.37 million at current prices.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Expands Infrastructure Financing Amid AI Boom
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Goldman Sachs is intensifying its efforts in the data center and infrastructure financing market to capitalize on the growing demand driven by the artificial intelligence surge. Sources indicate that the company is forming a dedicated team within its global banking and markets division to focus on global infrastructure financing. This initiative includes increasing lending in this sector and finding investors for these debts.

The move is fueled by a new wave of multi-billion-dollar transactions involving AI data centers, their significant energy requirements, and financing for processing units that support AI development. The new team will also concentrate on traditional infrastructure construction or upgrade projects in both developed and emerging markets.

Additionally, the team will be responsible for raising funds for renewable energy and certain types of liquefied natural gas projects. They will also provide financing for military and other equipment related to rising defense expenditures in various countries.

data.title: Bitget has launched U-margin ZBT perpetual contracts with a leverage range of 1-20x
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget has launched U-margined ZBT perpetual contracts, offering leverage ranging from 1x to 20x. Trading of BOT contracts will also be available simultaneously, along with participation in the CandyBomb event, which offers a total prize pool of 100,000 ZBT and ends at 21:30 (UTC+8) on October 24th.

data.title: An unknown hacker has lost over $8.617 million by buying high and selling low on ETH since October
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai, an unknown hacker has lost more than $8.617 million by buying high and selling low on ETH since October. After panic selling 8,637 ETH on October 11, losing $5.37 million, he sold another 7,817 ETH at an average price of 3,733 USDT during the decline today, losing $3.247 million (cost 4,148 USDT.95). An hour after the sale, he recovered another 2,815 ETH at a price of 3,783 USDT.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks fell across the board at the opening of the US stock market, with Coinbase falling 3.13%
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the US stock market opening, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.05%, the S&P 500 fell 0.18%, and the Nasdaq fell 0.38%. The crypto sector saw mixed gains and losses, with Bitmine (NASDAQ:BMNR) down 1.86%, Circle (NYSE:CRCL) down 2.16%, Coinbase down 3.13%, and Strategy down 0.89%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, including US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.description:

The group, which collectively serves more than 300,000 major clients, aims to roll out tokens within this fiscal year after conducting trial transfers […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/japan-yen-backed-stablecoins-launch/

data.title: Estimates show U.S. jobless claims fell to around 215,000 last week
data.description: Initial jobless claims in the United States fell last week, according to an analysis of unadjusted state-level claims data released during the US government shutdown. A Bloomberg analysis of the data indicates that initial claims were approximately 215,000 in the week ending October 11, down from an estimated 234,000 the previous week. While the US Department of Labor has not released a weekly unemployment report since September 25 due to the government shutdown, it still provides downloadable data for most states. This estimate uses the Bureau of Labor Statistics' weekly seasonal adjustment coefficients, which are released in advance. This method closely matches the official seasonally adjusted data when data from all states is available. However, the latest weekly data for Arizona, Massachusetts, Nevada, and Tennessee are missing, so the average of the previous four weeks is used instead. Continuing claims rose slightly to 1.93 million in the week ending October 4, higher than the 1.92 million estimated the previous week. (Jinshi)
